sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.15 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: It is known that for each prime number$p$there exists a series$a_1, a_2,$of words in two variables such that the finite group$G$has abelian Sylow$p 0.2ex$-sub\-groups if and only if$a_k(G)=1$for almost all$k$. For$p=2$such a series is known explicitly (R.\,Brandl, J.~Austral. Math. Soc., 31 (1981), 464--469). What about$p > 2$?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
